description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular wooden exterior and a prominent front-facing screen displaying a colorful static pattern. The front panel features two large, round control knobs on the right side, likely for adjusting volume and channel. The screen is bordered by a thick, gray bezel, and the television is topped with two long, angled metal antennas for signal reception. The sides and back are finished in a wood grain texture, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or modern features such as flat panels or slim bezels.
description: A retro box-shaped television with a wood grain exterior, gray bezel, two control knobs, and metal antennas.
description_view_0: Front view showing the screen with static, two control knobs, and the gray bezel.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wood grain texture and the protruding control knobs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full wood grain back and both metal antennas.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with wood grain texture and a single visible antenna.
attribute_bezel_design: thick gray bezel, matte finish, rectangular, no visible branding or ornamentation
attribute_control_button_layout: two large round knobs on the front right panel, vertically aligned
